input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-14T01:05:18.274Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your MAUI page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` watches the OS theme and swaps values automatically — no event handling needed.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

For code-behind or dynamic scenarios, use the extension method on any `BindableObject`:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
this.SetAppTheme(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // dark

myLabel.SetAppTheme(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Color.FromArgb("#F0F0F0"));

myButton.SetAppTheme(
    Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

## Key points

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Reacts to `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Live updates** | Both approaches update instantly when the user toggles the OS theme |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For reuse, define `Color` resources per theme in `App.xaml` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` inside `AppThemeBinding` |
| **Force a theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` to override the system setting |
